paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VII. Disjoint holes
summary: The question of whether all graphs are self-isolating was posed in arXiv:2202.09118, where the authors note they know very few graphs that have the self-isolating property and none that are known not to have it; the only connected graphs known to be self-isolating are complete graphs, paths of arbitrary length, and complete bipartite graphs. No follow-up paper resolving this question in either direction was found in a search of the post-2022 literature. The question remains fully open.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch of arXiv:2311.05719 confirms it is about clock-free graphs and tree decompositions (Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV); it does not mention self-isolating graphs or arXiv:2202.09118. The fuzzy match is a false positive.

Not fetched (5-call cap reached). Paper is in the same series (Polynomial bounds VI) and predates the self-isolating question; contribution description concerns good forests and P4-free graphs, not self-isolating graphs — likely a false positive.

notes: No follow-up found after 5 web calls (3 searches, 2 fetches). The self-isolating concept is specific to the polynomial chi-boundedness programme for H-free graphs where H is a forest; the question is whether every graph H has the self-isolating property, which would simplify the overall programme. The internal references are all false positives from fuzzy matching — none address the self-isolating question.
